---
title: "Online Books"
---

My summaries on what I have learned on econometrics and causal inference.

### [Econometrics Guide](https://xiangao.github.io/econometrics-guide/)

A practical guide to applied econometrics with examples in R and Stata.

### [Topics on econometrics and causal inference](https://xiangao.github.io/blog_book/)

Collected notes and posts on applied econometrics and causal inference, compiled into book format.

### [Causal Econometrics Guide](https://xiangao.github.io/causal_econometrics_guide/)

A guide to modern causal inference methods for applied researchers, with R implementations. Covers identification and potential outcomes (ATE, ATT, LATE, CATE, QTE on a single DGP), DAG-based identification and a graph-to-estimate pipeline using `causaleffect` and `dagitty`, sensitivity analysis (Cinelli-Hazlett robustness values, E-values, Rosenbaum bounds), estimation (RA, IPW, AIPW, IPWRA, TMLE, DoubleML, propensity score / full / coarsened-exact matching via `MatchIt`, heterogeneous treatment effects via meta-learners and causal forests in `grf`, policy trees, continuous-treatment dose-response via generalized propensity scores and `lmtp`, Bayesian causal inference via BART and BCF), difference-in-differences, IV and regression discontinuity, shift-share (Bartik) IV, Poisson IV, longitudinal causal inference (parametric g-formula, IPTW + marginal structural models, longitudinal TMLE via `ltmle`), survival analysis with causal inference (Cox PH, IPCW, RMST differences, doubly-robust survival via `riskRegression`), mediation, and causal discovery (PC, GES, FCI, RFCI).

### [Causal Econometrics in Julia](https://xiangao.github.io/causal_econometrics_julia/)

A hands-on guide to causal inference and econometrics using Julia, covering identification, potential outcomes, five estimands on one DGP, sensitivity analysis (Cinelli-Hazlett robustness values, E-values, Rosenbaum bounds), estimation (RA, IPW, AIPW, TMLE, DoubleML, propensity score matching and balance diagnostics, heterogeneous treatment effects via S/T/X/R/DR meta-learners with `MLJ` + `DecisionTree`, continuous-treatment dose-response via Hirano-Imbens GPS and Kennedy DR, Bayesian g-computation), distributional treatment effects, difference-in-differences (ETWFE, synthetic control, synthetic DiD, TASC), shift-share (Bartik) IV with Rotemberg weights and BHJ shock-level inference, IV and regression discontinuity, Poisson IV, longitudinal causal inference (g-formula + IPTW + marginal structural models), survival analysis (Kaplan-Meier, IPW-adjusted survival curves, restricted mean survival time), mediation, and causal discovery (PC, RSL-D, FCI, L-MARVEL) with a graph-to-estimate pipeline via `CausalGraphs.jl`.
